facts_disclosed: Quit smoking — 3 months clean, nicotine patches

turns.content: can't sleep again. but for once it's not anxiety it's just my brain being a brain. wanted to talk to someone
I'm here! What's on your mind tonight?

honestly? something good for once. I hit three months yesterday without a single cigarette parcero. THREE MONTHS. I almost didn't notice because I was working but lucía reminded me and made this whole little celebration at dinner
That's a huge deal — congratulations! Three months is a real milestone, the first few months are the hardest part. How are you feeling physically?

so much better parce. I can run for the bus without dying. my sense of smell came back which is honestly a mixed blessing in bogotá lol but I can taste food again. I'm still using nicotine patches though. tried cold turkey before and lasted maybe eleven days before lucía basically staged an intervention like please go back to nicotine in some form

There's zero shame in patches — the "cold turkey is the only real way" thing is a myth. Patches handle the physical withdrawal so you can focus on breaking the behavioral and emotional habits, which is the harder part anyway. Did you have any support beyond the patches?

my therapist actually. she helped me see it differently. she said it was the first real boundary I'd ever set with myself. which hit me kind of hard because she's right. I spent years trying to quit with this all-or-nothing mentality and it never stuck. this time the goal is just zero actual cigarettes and that's DONE. the patches can come later

That's a really powerful reframe — quitting as a boundary with yourself, not just a health decision but an act of self-respect. And your approach is smart. Zero cigarettes for three months IS the win. Everything else is optimization.

and the thing is I notice it in my work too? I used to smoke when I was stuck on a paragraph or waiting for a callback and now I just... sit with it. turns out the cigarette never actually helped me think, it just gave me something to do with my hands while I panicked

That's a huge insight. A lot of people think smoking is part of their creative process but it's really just a way to manage discomfort without processing it. The fact that you can sit with that discomfort now — that changes more than just the smoking habit. That kind of thing bleeds into everything.
okay you're going to make me cry at 2am and I have a deadline tomorrow so let's not lol. but seriously. three months. I keep saying it because I can't believe it. gracias parcero. buenas noches
Buenas noches, Valentina. Three months and counting — you earned it. Go get some sleep and crush that deadline.
